hook.text: English is spoken in over 60 countries. That's not because it's the best language — it's because of empire.

buildup.text: Linguistic imperialism is the dominance of one language over others through colonialism, economics, and education.

discovery.text: Colonial powers forced their languages on subjects. English, French, Spanish, and Portuguese replaced local tongues.

twist.text: Today, English dominance continues through business, tech, and academia — even without direct colonial control.

climax.text: Linguistic imperialism doesn't just spread words. It spreads worldviews, marginalizing alternative ways of thinking.

punchline.text: A dominant language carries a dominant worldview.

quiz.question: What is linguistic imperialism?
options: One language dominating others through power and institutions
A natural process of language evolution
